toolConfig.name: URL Encoder / Decoder
toolConfig.shortDesc: Encode special characters for URLs and decode them back
toolConfig.longDesc:
URL encoding (percent-encoding) converts characters that are not allowed in URLs into a `%xx` format. For example, space becomes `%20`, `&` becomes `%26`. This is essential when building query strings or passing parameters via links.

Our tool uses `encodeURIComponent` and `decodeURIComponent` which encode all reserved characters except letters, digits, and `-_.!~*'()` – that's the standard for modern web applications.

Features:
- One-click encode/decode.
- Swap button to quickly re-encode or re-decode.
- Handles UTF-8 characters (e.g., emojis, accents).
- No server interaction – 100% client-side.

Common scenarios:
- Preparing a search query to include in a URL: `q=hello world` → `q=hello%20world`.
- Decoding a callback parameter from OAuth redirects.
- Escaping special characters in redirect URIs.

Bookmark this tool – you'll reach for it every time you debug malformed URLs or build API clients.
toolConfig.seoTitle: URL Encoder & Decoder - Encode/Decode URLs Online
toolConfig.seoDescription: URL encode or decode strings instantly. Free online tool for percent-encoding special characters. Supports UTF-8, works fully in browser.
